checks.sources_list: still points at archive.ubuntu.com and security.ubuntu.com, both dead for xenial, never repointed to old-releases.ubuntu.com
checks.esm_channels: apt-cache policy lists esm.ubuntu.com xenial-infra-updates and xenial-infra-security (priority -32768, present but not preferred) -- Ubuntu Pro/ESM appears attached
checks.last_login: wtmp begins at this boot; no login history prior to it

notes: fresh boot, sources.list still names the dead public archive rather than old-releases, but ESM channels are visible in apt policy suggesting a Pro attachment is quietly keeping this xenial box on life support past its official death

checks.clock_skew: VM reads 2026-07-09, host reads 2026-07-10 (~1 day behind)

notes: young boot after three reboots in one hour; the siege log is nearly empty only because auth.log resets each boot, and the VM clock has drifted a full day behind the host it lives on

checks.clock_skew: none measurable this cycle -- VM and host both read 2026-07-09T19:32Z
checks.top_users_tried: admin, ubnt, supervisor, luca, debian, baduser
checks.oldest_proc: pid 1 systemd, 1579s (== uptime; nothing survived the reboots)

tarot.reading: Three reboots in an hour and now a fourth young boot: the Wheel turns but the axle is bent, always returning to minute zero. Reversed, it is not fate advancing but fate stuck -- uptime resets, auth.log resets, every process an orphan of the last cycle. The mirror still answers 200 from a Date header dated yesterday, indifferent to which turn of the wheel we are on.

notes: young 25-min boot after last night's reboot spree; 12 invalid-user probes already knocking (admin, ubnt, luca) but fail2ban hasn't crossed its ban threshold, and no clock skew this cycle -- VM and host agree on 2026-07-09T19:32Z

checks.old_releases_ubuntu_com: resolves, HTTP 404 on the specific dists/xenial/Release path probed -- host answers, that exact path came back empty
checks.invalid_users_tried: unknown, ubnt, supervisor, postgres, luca, debian, baduser, admin

tarot.reading: Load average flat at 0.00 across the board, zero interactive users, seventeen hours unbroken since last night's reboot cluster ended: the machine has gone quiet and inward. Five IPs sit banned in fail2ban's memory like old grudges, but tonight nobody currently knocks -- the Hermit's lantern is lit for no one, on a road nobody official maintains anymore.

notes: longest stable uptime yet (17h) after last night's reboot cluster; siege is cumulative and modest (137 failed logins total, 5 ever banned, 0 banned right now) and disk/mem/services are all quietly fine

checks.old_releases_ubuntu_com: resolves fine, but repos are still being served from archive.ubuntu.com/security.ubuntu.com plus esm.ubuntu.com -- old-releases wasn't even needed this cycle
checks.top_attacker: no repeat offenders yet in the fresh log -- 8 distinct IPs, one hit each (92.255.196.185, 59.34.17.130, 220.80.223.144, ...)
checks.invalid_users_tried: unknown, ubnt, supervisor, postgres, luca, debian, baduser, admin

tarot.reading: Fail2ban's ledger reads 165 failed attempts and only 5 ever banned -- a reckoning perpetually deferred, not delivered. The five candidate packages refused to rise when apt called them; today's judgement is a no-op, the trumpet sounded to an empty room.

actions_taken: sudo apt-get upgrade -y (0 packages actually upgraded; 5 held back)
notes: apt-get upgrade ran clean but all 5 candidate packages were held back (dependency-kept, nothing installed); sshd already hardened (PermitRootLogin prohibit-password, PasswordAuthentication no); no zombies, no swap pressure, only port 22 listening; auth.log rotated since last cycle so today's siege count looks light but is the same slow drip

checks.invalid_users_tried: ubnt, supervisor, postgres, luca, debian, baduser, admin
checks.ufw_status: inactive (left untouched -- not flipping firewall state on a box only reachable via SSH)

tarot.reading: Total banned climbs to twenty, total failed to three hundred sixty-two, and still only one IP sits in the box right now: 91.92.40.5, this cycle's turn on the wheel. The five packages that never upgrade spin in place too, held back cycle after cycle, going nowhere in particular.

actions_taken: sudo apt-get upgrade -y (0 packages actually upgraded; same 5 held back)
notes: Saturday, daily allowance only; two-day uptime holds, no zombies, no swap pressure, sshd already hardened; ufw inactive but left alone; fail2ban has banned 20 IPs lifetime, 1 currently (91.92.40.5); apt upgrade a no-op again
